name: St. Paul’s Cathedral
description:
St. Paul’s Cathedral is an architectural masterpiece known for its large dome and historic significance. Visitors can explore the interior, climb to the Whispering Gallery, and enjoy views from the dome. The cathedral has hosted important events, including royal ceremonies. Its design and history make it a key cultural landmark in London.

The magnificent St. Paul’s Cathedral is one of London’s most iconic landmarks, famous for its grand dome, historic significance, and stunning architecture.

Why St. Paul’s Cathedral is special

  • Designed by Sir Christopher Wren
  • Completed in 1710 after the Great Fire of London
  • One of the largest cathedral domes in the world
  • A symbol of resilience during WWII

Top Highlights

The Dome

  • One of the most recognizable features of London
  • Climb to the top for incredible city views

Whispering Gallery

  • Unique acoustic feature—whispers travel around the dome

Golden Gallery

  • Highest viewing platform with panoramic views of London

Historical significance

  • Site of major events:
    • Wedding of Prince Charles and Princess Diana
    • State funerals of notable figures

Things to do

  • Explore the cathedral interior
  • Climb the dome for views
  • Visit the crypt and tombs

Visiting tips

  • Arrive early to avoid crowds
  • Be prepared for stairs if climbing the dome
  • Check service times (restricted access during services)

How long to spend

  • 1.5–2 hours

Location

  • Near:
    • Millennium Bridge
    • Tate Modern

Travel tips

  • Wear comfortable shoes (lots of steps)
  • Photography rules apply inside
  • Combine with a Thames walk

Fun fact

St. Paul’s Cathedral remained standing during the Blitz in WWII, becoming a symbol of hope for Londoners.

name: Brera District
description:
Brera is a charming district known for its artistic heritage, narrow streets, and bohemian atmosphere. It is home to the Brera Art Gallery, which houses important Italian artworks. The area features boutiques, cafés, and antique shops. Visitors can stroll through its streets, enjoying architecture and local culture. Brera offers a more relaxed and cultural experience compared to Milan’s modern districts.

The Brera District is Milan’s most charming and artistic neighborhood, often called the city’s bohemian heart. With cobblestone streets, art studios, boutique shops, and lively cafés, it offers a perfect mix of culture and relaxed elegance.

What Makes Brera Special

Artistic Soul of Milan

  • Home to the famous Pinacoteca di Brera
  • Long history as a hub for artists, writers, and intellectuals
  • Street performers and galleries add to its creative vibe

Beautiful Streets & Atmosphere

  • Narrow lanes like Via Brera and Via Fiori Chiari
  • Colorful buildings, balconies, and ivy-covered walls
  • Perfect for slow walks and photography 📸

Cafés, Restaurants & Boutiques

  • Stylish cafés and fine dining spots
  • Independent boutiques and artisan shops
  • Great place for Italian aperitivo culture 🍸

Things to Do

  • Visit art galleries and studios
  • Enjoy coffee or wine in a street-side café
  • Shop for unique fashion and handmade goods
  • Explore hidden courtyards and alleys

Best Time to Visit

  • Daytime: For art, shopping, and exploring
  • Evening: For lively dining and romantic ambiance

Location & Access

  • Located near Milan city center
  • Walking distance from Duomo and Sforza Castle

Why Visit Brera District

Brera offers a different side of Milan—less about grand monuments and more about atmosphere, creativity, and lifestyle. It’s the perfect place to feel the city’s soul.

name: Dragon’s Back
description:
Dragon’s Back offers a ridge walk with sea views, green slopes, and access to beaches. Top attractions include the trail and nearby Shek O. Visitors can experience one of Hong Kong’s most popular hikes. Key highlights include accessible panoramic hiking. Things to do include trekking.

Dragon’s Back is Hong Kong’s most famous hike—loved for its easy-to-moderate trail, sweeping ridge views, and stunning coastal scenery. It’s perfect if you want a rewarding hike without extreme difficulty.

Where is it?

  • On the southeastern side of Hong Kong Island
  • Part of the longer Hong Kong Trail (Section 8)
  • Ends near Shek O and Big Wave Bay

What makes it special?

The “dragon spine” ridge

  • Rolling hills that resemble a dragon’s back
  • One of the most scenic ridgelines in Hong Kong

Ocean views

  • Panoramic views of the South China Sea
  • Overlooks beaches like Shek O and Big Wave Bay

Beginner-friendly adventure

  • Relatively easy compared to other hikes
  • Well-marked trail, great for casual hikers

Trail highlights

The ridge walk

  • The most iconic part of the hike
  • Open views on both sides

Big Wave Bay finish

  • End your hike at a beach
  • Perfect for a swim or relaxing break

Hike details

  • Duration: ~2–3 hours
  • Difficulty: Easy to moderate
  • Best route: To Tei Wan → Dragon’s Back → Big Wave Bay

Best time to hike

  • October–April: Cool, clear weather 
  • Early morning or late afternoon: Best light and temperature

Tips

  • Bring water and sun protection 
  • Wear good walking shoes
  • Avoid midday heat in summer
  • Check weather before hiking

Why hike Dragon’s Back?

It offers the perfect mix of accessibility and breathtaking views—making it one of the best hikes not just in Hong Kong, but in all of Asia.

name: Herat
description: Herat preserves citadels, minarets, and Timurid-era cultural heritage central to Persianate history. Top attractions include Herat Citadel, Friday Mosque, and surviving minaret complexes. Visitors can explore one of the region’s great historic cities. Key highlights include architectural heritage and artistic traditions. Things to do include walking tours and heritage exploration.

name: Painted Monasteries of Bucovina
description: Bucovina’s painted monasteries preserve vivid exterior frescoes unique in Orthodox art. Top attractions include Voroneț, Sucevița, and Moldovița monasteries. Visitors can explore religious sites where painted walls narrate sacred stories. Key highlights include artistic heritage and rural settings. Things to do include monastery touring.

name: Zokhawthar
description: Zokhawthar is a border village near Myanmar, offering scenic views and cultural experiences. Visitors can explore markets, rivers, and local traditions. It provides a glimpse into cross-border culture and lifestyle. The village is peaceful and picturesque.

name: Princep Ghat
description:
Princep Ghat is a historic riverside spot known for its colonial architecture. Visitors can enjoy river views, boating, and evening walks. The illuminated bridge nearby adds to the beauty. It is a popular place for relaxation and photography. 

Princep Ghat is one of the most iconic and scenic spots in Kolkata, located along the banks of the Hooghly River. Built in 1841 during British rule, it’s famous for its elegant white Palladian-style monument dedicated to James Prinsep, a scholar and antiquarian.

Why it’s special

  • Historic architecture: The Greek-style columns make it one of Kolkata’s most photographed landmarks
  • Riverfront views: Perfect for sunsets with the Hooghly and the majestic Vidyasagar Setu in the background
  • Relaxing vibe: A popular hangout for locals, couples, and tourists
  • Cultural charm: Often used for film shoots, photoshoots, and evening strolls

Things to do

  • Take a peaceful walk along the riverside promenade
  • Enjoy a boat ride on the Hooghly River
  • Sit and watch the sunset under the bridge
  • Try local street snacks like jhal muri or ice cream
  • Capture beautiful photographs, especially at dusk

Best time to visit

  • Evening (4:30 PM – 7:30 PM) for sunset views
  • Winter months (Nov–Feb) offer the most pleasant weather

How to reach

  • Nearest railway station: Prinsep Ghat station (circular railway)
  • Metro: Maidan or Esplanade, then a short ride
  • Easily accessible by taxi, auto, or bus

name: Burj Al Arab
description:
Burj Al Arab is one of the most iconic and luxurious hotels in the world, located in Dubai. Known for its sail-shaped design, it stands on its own artificial island and symbolizes Dubai’s luxury and innovation.

What Makes It Special

  • Famous for its unique sail-like architecture
  • Often described as the world’s only “7-star hotel” (unofficial title)
  • Built on a man-made island connected by a private bridge

Luxury Experience

Grand Interiors

  • Lavish interiors decorated with gold leaf, marble, and vibrant colors
  • One of the tallest hotel atriums in the world

Fine Dining

  • World-class restaurants with panoramic views
  • Signature dining experiences and afternoon tea

Exclusive Services

  • Private butlers, chauffeur-driven Rolls-Royce transfers
  • Helipad used for special events and luxury arrivals

Best Ways to Experience It

  • Book afternoon tea or dining for inside access
  • Enjoy views from nearby Jumeirah Beach
  • Visit Souk Madinat Jumeirah for scenic photo spots

Location

  • Situated in the Jumeirah area, along Dubai’s coastline
  • Around 15–20 minutes from Downtown Dubai

Best Time to Visit

  • Sunset for beautiful lighting and views
  • Night for a fully illuminated, iconic look

Why Visit?

  • One of the most luxurious and recognizable hotels in the world
  • A symbol of Dubai’s wealth, design, and ambition
  • Perfect for photography and once-in-a-lifetime experiences

name: Cooch Behar Palace
description: Cooch Behar Palace is a grand royal residence inspired by European architecture. It features beautiful gardens and interiors. Visitors can explore the palace museum and learn about royal history. The palace is a major attraction in North Bengal.

name: Bucegi Mountains
description: Bucegi combines rocky plateaus, cable-car access, and iconic formations such as the Sphinx. Top attractions include mountain trails, viewpoints, and alpine meadows. Visitors can experience highland landscapes close to major Transylvanian towns. Key highlights include mountain scenery and unusual rock forms. Things to do include hiking and photography.

name: Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque
description:
The Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque is one of the most breathtaking landmarks in Abu Dhabi and among the largest mosques in the world. It was built in honor of Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan, the founding father of the UAE.

What Makes It Special

  • Over 80 white marble domes and stunning minarets
  • One of the world’s largest hand-knotted carpets
  • Massive crystal chandeliers adorned with gold and Swarovski crystals
  • Reflecting pools that create magical mirror-like views

Architectural Beauty

  • Combines Islamic, Mughal, and Moorish styles
  • Intricate floral designs made with semi-precious stones
  • Pure white marble that changes color with the light

Unique Experience

  • The mosque looks different throughout the day
  • At night, it is illuminated with soft blue lighting reflecting moon phases

Location

  • About 20 minutes from Abu Dhabi city center
  • Easily accessible by taxi or guided tours

Best Time to Visit

  • Sunset for a mix of daylight and evening views
  • Evening for peaceful atmosphere and stunning lighting
  • Early morning for fewer crowds

Dress Code

  • Modest clothing required
  • Women: headscarf, long sleeves, full-length clothing
  • Men: long pants, no sleeveless shirts

Why Visit?

  • One of the most beautiful religious sites in the world
  • A symbol of peace, culture, and Islamic architecture
  • Free entry and guided tours available

name: Kidepo Valley National Park
description: Kidepo combines broad savannas, mountain backdrops, and one of East Africa’s most remote safari settings. Top attractions include game routes and scenic valleys. Visitors can experience dramatic wilderness far from busier circuits. Key highlights include remoteness and predator-rich landscapes. Things to do include safari drives and photography.

name: Burren
description: The Burren combines limestone pavements, wildflowers, and archaeological remains across unusual terrain. Top attractions include Poulnabrone Dolmen, cave systems, and scenic routes. Visitors can explore one of Europe’s most distinctive karst landscapes. Key highlights include geology and ancient heritage. Things to do include hiking and heritage visits.

name: Bornholm
description:
ornholm is a distinctive Danish island in the Baltic Sea, geographically closer to Sweden than mainland Denmark. It feels different from the rest of the country—more rugged, sunnier, and deeply connected to nature and local traditions.

Why Bornholm is special

  • Known as Denmark’s sunniest island
  • Unique coastline: rocky cliffs (north) + sandy beaches (south)
  • One of Europe’s best islands for cycling
  • Strong culture of crafts, glasswork, and local food

Top highlights

Hammershus Castle Ruins

  • Hammershus
  • Largest medieval fortress ruins in Northern Europe
  • Dramatic cliffside setting with sweeping sea views

Round Churches

  • Østerlars Church
  • Unique circular churches used for both worship and defense
  • One of Bornholm’s most iconic landmarks

Dueodde Beach

  • Dueodde Beach
  • Famous for ultra-fine, white sand
  • Often compared to tropical beaches

Gudhjem Village

  • Gudhjem
  • Charming harbor town with steep streets
  • Known for smoked fish and sea views

Things to do

  • Cycle around the island
  • Explore cliffs, forests, and beaches 
  • Visit artisan workshops and markets 
  • Taste local specialties (especially smoked herring) 

Best time to visit

  • June–August: best weather 
  • May & September: quieter and still pleasant

Getting there

  • Ferry from Denmark or Sweden
  • Flights available from Copenhagen

Travel tip

Plan at least 2–3 days—Bornholm is best enjoyed slowly, with time to explore different parts of the island.

Bornholm offers a rare mix of wild coastal beauty, peaceful villages, and creative culture—perfect for a relaxed Scandinavian escape.

name: Tawang Monastery
description: Tawang Monastery is the largest monastery in India and one of the most important Buddhist centers in the region. Built in the 17th century, it houses a massive statue of Buddha and ancient scriptures. The monastery offers panoramic views of the surrounding mountains. Visitors can experience peaceful surroundings, prayer ceremonies, and cultural heritage. It plays a significant role in the spiritual life of the Monpa community.

name: Nartiang Monoliths
description: Nartiang Monoliths are ancient stone structures believed to be the largest collection of monoliths in India. They hold historical and cultural significance for the Jaintia tribe. Visitors can explore the site and learn about tribal history. The monoliths reflect ancient traditions and architecture.

name: Leuven
description: Leuven is a lively university city known for its historic architecture and vibrant student life The Town Hall features intricate Gothic design while the university adds cultural energy Visitors can explore cafés libraries and parks Leuven combines history with youthful atmosphere Visitors enjoy medieval architecture culture chocolate beer history canals and vibrant European experiences throughout Belgium. name: Ujjayanta Palace
description: Ujjayanta Palace is one of the most iconic landmarks in Tripura, located in the capital city of Agartala. Built by Maharaja Radha Kishore Manikya in the early 20th century, the palace showcases Indo-Saracenic architecture with beautiful domes, gardens, and water bodies. Today, it houses the Tripura State Museum, displaying artifacts, sculptures, tribal culture, and history. Visitors can explore galleries that highlight the rich heritage of the state. The palace is surrounded by Mughal-style gardens and illuminated beautifully at night, creating a majestic atmosphere. Ujjayanta Palace reflects the royal legacy and cultural richness of Tripura, making it a must-visit destination for history and architecture enthusiasts.

name: Charles Bridge
description:
Charles Bridge is a historic stone bridge connecting Old Town and Lesser Town Built in the 14th century it is lined with statues of saints and offers scenic views of the river Street artists musicians and vendors create a lively atmosphere making it one of Prague’s most iconic landmarks Visitors enjoy medieval architecture castles bridges culture history cafés and vibrant city life throughout Prague.

Charles Bridge is Prague’s most famous landmark—a stunning medieval bridge lined with statues, connecting the Old Town with the castle district.

Location

  • Spans the Vltava River
  • Links Old Town Square (via Old Town) with Malá Strana

History & Origins

  • Construction began in 1357 under Charles IV
  • Built to replace an earlier bridge destroyed by floods
  • Served as the main connection across the river for centuries

Why It’s Special

  • One of the most beautiful bridges in Europe
  • Lined with 30 Baroque statues of saints
  • A symbol of Prague’s medieval heritage

Highlights

Statues of Saints

  • Famous statue of Saint John of Nepomuk
  • Tradition says touching it brings good luck

Bridge Towers

  • Old Town Bridge Tower (one of the finest Gothic gateways in Europe)
  • Lesser Town towers on the opposite side

Street Performers & Artists

  • Musicians, painters, and vendors create a lively atmosphere

Best Experiences

  • Sunrise: peaceful, magical, almost empty
  • Sunset: golden views over the river
  • Night: beautifully lit, romantic atmosphere

Photography Tips

  • Best shots from:
    • Riverside viewpoints
    • Bridge towers
    • Early morning for no crowds

Travel Tips

  • Very crowded during the day—go early or late
  • Watch your belongings
  • Combine with:
    • Prague Castle
    • Kampa Island

Why Visit Charles Bridge?

It’s not just a crossing—it’s a living gallery of history, art, and atmosphere, offering some of the most memorable views in Prague.

name: Arusha
description: Arusha combines market streets, volcanic views, and its role as gateway to northern Tanzania’s great parks. Top attractions include city cultural sites, coffee estates, and nearby natural excursions. Visitors can explore a lively highland city between safari journeys. Key highlights include regional gateway identity and Mount Meru backdrop. Things to do include city tours and cultural visits.

name: Tian Tan Buddha
description:
Tian Tan Buddha combines mountain scenery, monastery heritage, and one of Hong Kong’s major spiritual landmarks. Top attractions include Po Lin Monastery, Buddha steps, and Ngong Ping viewpoints. Visitors can pair cultural exploration with mountain landscapes. Key highlights include sacred atmosphere and elevated scenery. Things to do include cable car rides and heritage visits.

Tian Tan Buddha—often called the Big Buddha—is one of Hong Kong’s most iconic landmarks. Perched high on Lantau Island, it’s a powerful symbol of peace, harmony, and spirituality.

Why It’s Special

  • One of the largest seated outdoor Buddha statues in the world
  • Made of bronze and standing about 34 meters tall
  • Faces north toward mainland China—symbolizing unity
  • Surrounded by six smaller statues offering symbolic tributes

The 268 Steps Experience

  • To reach the Buddha, you’ll climb 268 steps
  • The climb is gradual, with rest spots along the way
  • At the top: panoramic views of mountains, sea, and sky 🌄
  • The peaceful atmosphere makes the climb feel meaningful, not tiring

Po Lin Monastery Nearby


Right next to the Buddha is the serene
Po Lin Monastery:

  • One of Hong Kong’s most important Buddhist sites
  • Known for its traditional architecture and incense-filled halls
  • Don’t miss the famous vegetarian lunch served here 🥗

Getting There — A Highlight Itself

  • Take the scenic Ngong Ping 360
    • 25-minute ride over mountains and sea
    • Optional glass-bottom cabin for extra thrill
  • Or take a bus from Tung Chung if you prefer

Best Time to Visit

  • Early morning → fewer crowds, peaceful vibe
  • Late afternoon → beautiful golden light for photos
  • Avoid weekends if you want a quieter experience

Tips for Visiting

  • Wear comfortable shoes for the climb
  • Bring water, especially in warmer months
  • Combine with Tai O fishing village or a Lantau hike

Cultural Note

  • Dress modestly (it’s a religious site)
  • Be respectful—this is an active place of worship

The Tian Tan Buddha isn’t just a sightseeing spot—it’s a place where nature, culture, and spirituality come together. Standing beneath it, you’ll feel both the scale of the statue and the calm of the surroundings.

name: Mawlamyine
description: Mawlamyine combines riverfront scenery, pagodas, and literary-era colonial streets. Top attractions include ridge viewpoints, pagodas, and old-town areas. Visitors can explore one of lower Myanmar’s historic cities with a distinctive river setting. Key highlights include cultural heritage and scenic outlooks. Things to do include walking tours and food exploration.

name: Buckingham Palace
description:
Buckingham Palace is the official residence of the British monarch and one of London’s most iconic landmarks. The palace features grand architecture, ceremonial rooms, and expansive gardens. Visitors gather to witness the Changing of the Guard ceremony, a display of British tradition and precision. During certain times of the year, parts of the palace are open to the public, allowing exploration of its lavish interiors and royal collections. Located in central London, it is surrounded by parks and monuments, making it a key highlight of the city’s royal heritage.

The iconic Buckingham Palace is the official residence of the British monarch and one of the most famous landmarks in London.

Why Buckingham Palace is special

  • Official home of the British monarch
  • A symbol of the UK’s royal heritage
  • Used for state ceremonies and official events
  • One of the most visited attractions in London

Top Experiences

Changing of the Guard

  • Famous ceremonial event with guards in red uniforms
  • Includes marching bands and traditional pageantry
  • Usually takes place in the morning (check schedule)

State Rooms (Seasonal Opening)

  • Open to visitors in summer
  • Lavish rooms with royal art, furniture, and decorations

The Royal Grounds

  • Beautiful gardens and surroundings
  • View from outside includes the Victoria Memorial

What to do

  • Watch the Changing of the Guard
  • Take photos at the gates and palace front
  • Walk through nearby St. James’s Park

Best time to visit

  • Morning for the guard ceremony
  • Early arrival for good viewing spots
  • Summer if you want to enter the palace

Getting there

  • Nearest stations:
    • Victoria
    • Green Park
    • St. James’s Park

How long to spend

  • 30–60 minutes outside
  • 2–3 hours if visiting State Rooms

Travel tips

  • Arrive early for the best view of the ceremony
  • Check official schedules before visiting
  • Combine with nearby attractions like Westminster

Fun fact

Buckingham Palace has 775 rooms, including 19 State Rooms and 78 bathrooms!

name: Marine National Park
description: Marine National Park in Jamnagar is India’s first marine sanctuary. It features coral reefs, mangroves, and diverse marine life. Visitors can explore tidal areas and observe marine species. The park offers a unique ecological experience and is ideal for nature lovers.

name: Kondapalli Fort
description: Kondapalli Fort is a historic fort offering views of surrounding landscapes. It features ancient structures and history related to various dynasties. Visitors can explore the fort and enjoy scenic beauty.

name: Egeskov Castle
description:
Egeskov Castle is one of Europe’s best-preserved Renaissance water castles, located on the island of Funen. Built in 1554, it looks like a true fairytale fortress—rising directly out of a lake and surrounded by lush gardens.

Why it’s so special

  • Built on oak piles in the middle of a lake (hence the name “Egeskov” = oak forest)
  • One of the best-preserved Renaissance castles in Europe
  • Famous for its award-winning gardens and parklands
  • Still privately owned and lived in

Highlights

The Castle Itself

  • Twin buildings connected by a thick wall (for defense)
  • Surrounded by a moat, accessible by a bridge
  • Interiors filled with historic furniture, art, and royal rooms

Gardens & Park

  • Beautifully landscaped gardens with:
    • Flower beds
    • Hedge mazes and labyrinths
    • Seasonal displays
  • Perfect for long, relaxing walks

Unique Experiences

  • Tree-top walking (suspension walkway through the forest)
  • Vintage car and motorcycle exhibitions 🚗
  • Family-friendly outdoor activities

Things to do

  • Explore the castle rooms and towers
  • Walk around the lake for reflections 📸
  • Get lost in the garden maze 🌿
  • Enjoy picnics in the park

Best time to visit

  • Spring–Summer: gardens in full bloom 🌸
  • Autumn: colorful foliage 🍂
  • Closed or limited access in winter

Getting there

  • About 30 minutes from Odense
  • Around 2 hours from Copenhagen

Travel tip

Plan to spend at least half a day—the gardens and activities are just as impressive as the castle itself.

Egeskov Castle is a perfect blend of history, nature, and fairytale beauty, making it one of Denmark’s most magical destinations.

name: Sri Kalahasti Temple
description: Sri Kalahasti Temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva and is known for Rahu-Ketu dosha rituals. The temple features Dravidian architecture and spiritual significance. It is an important pilgrimage destination.

name: FIFA World Football Museum
description:
The FIFA World Football Museum is a modern attraction dedicated to the history and global impact of football. Located near Lake Zurich, the museum features interactive exhibits, multimedia displays, and memorabilia from international tournaments. Visitors can explore the evolution of the sport, iconic moments, and the stories of legendary players and teams. The highlight includes displays of FIFA World Cup trophies and immersive experiences that engage fans of all ages. The museum also offers games, simulations, and educational exhibits, making it both entertaining and informative. It reflects football’s role as a unifying global sport and provides a unique experience in Zurich beyond traditional sightseeing.

The FIFA World Football Museum is a must-visit destination for football fans—celebrating the history, culture, and global impact of the world’s most popular sport.

Why it’s special

  • Official museum of FIFA
  • Covers over 3,000 m² of exhibition space
  • Showcases the story of football from its origins to modern times
  • Highly interactive and family-friendly

Top Highlights

FIFA World Cup Gallery

  • Displays original trophies and memorabilia
  • Features shirts from all FIFA member nations

Interactive Zones

  • Test your football skills (passing, shooting, reactions)
  • Fun games and digital experiences

Multimedia Exhibits

  • Iconic World Cup moments
  • Emotional stories of players, teams, and fans

What you’ll learn

  • Evolution of football rules and tactics
  • History of the FIFA World Cup
  • Cultural impact of football worldwide
  • Legendary players and unforgettable matches

Visiting info

  • Location: Central Zurich, near Enge station
  • Open most days (usually closed Monday—check ahead)

How long to spend

  • 1.5–2 hours for a quick visit
  • 3+ hours if you explore everything

Getting there

  • Easily accessible from Zurich city center
  • Well connected by tram and train

Travel tips

  • Great for kids and non-football fans too
  • Combine with a Zurich city tour or lake visit
  • Try the museum café and shop for football souvenirs

Fun fact

The museum includes memorabilia from every FIFA member country, highlighting how football connects the entire world.

name: Erawan Shrine
description:
Erawan Shrine is a Hindu shrine dedicated to Lord Brahma. It is known for spiritual significance and cultural performances. Visitors pray for good fortune. The shrine provides a peaceful space in a busy area.

Erawan Shrine (Erawan Shrine), also called the Shrine of Phra Phrom, is one of Bangkok’s most famous spiritual landmarks. It is dedicated to the four-faced Brahma and is visited by both locals and travelers seeking blessings for luck, success, health, and protection.

Highlights

  • Beautiful golden four-faced Brahma statue
  • Constant atmosphere of prayer, incense, and flower offerings
  • Traditional Thai classical dance performances offered by devotees
  • A unique blend of Hindu and Thai spiritual traditions

Things to do

  • Observe or participate in making offerings
  • Watch traditional dancers perform beside the shrine
  • Experience one of Bangkok’s busiest devotional sites
  • Visit nearby shopping landmarks after the shrine

Location

  • In the Ratchaprasong district, beside the Grand Hyatt Erawan
  • Easy access from BTS Chit Lom
  • Near major malls like CentralWorld and Siam Paragon

Cultural note

Many worshippers pray to each face of Brahma in turn, traditionally for:

  1. Career / success
  2. Relationships
  3. Health
  4. Protection

Best time to visit

  • Morning for a quieter spiritual atmosphere
  • Evening for lights and lively devotional activity

Why visit

Erawan Shrine is a fascinating stop for culture, spirituality, and people-watching, and it shows a very different side of Bangkok from temples and markets.

name: Global Vipassana Pagoda
description: Global Vipassana Pagoda is a meditation center known for its large dome structure. It promotes the practice of Vipassana meditation. Visitors can explore the pagoda and learn about mindfulness. The peaceful environment makes it ideal for spiritual seekers.

name: Panjshir Valley
description: Panjshir combines river-cut valleys, steep mountain walls, and villages framed by the Hindu Kush. Top attractions include valley routes and scenic viewpoints. Visitors can experience one of Afghanistan’s most striking mountain corridors. Key highlights include dramatic landscapes and river scenery. Things to do include scenic drives and trekking.

name: Trongsa Dzong
description: Trongsa Dzong is strategically located on a ridge overlooking a deep valley. It played a key role in Bhutan’s history and is associated with the royal family. The dzong features multiple levels, courtyards, and temples. Visitors can explore its architecture and learn about Bhutan’s past. The surrounding landscape adds to its grandeur. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value monasteries landscapes culture and peaceful Himalayan atmosphere. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value monasteries landscapes culture and peaceful Himalayan atmosphere. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value monasteries landscapes culture and peaceful Himalayan atmosphere. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value monasteries landscapes culture and peaceful Himalayan atmosphere. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value monasteries landscapes culture and peaceful Himalayan atmosphere. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value monasteries landscapes culture and peaceful Himalayan atmosphere. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value monasteries landscapes culture and peaceful Himalayan atmosphere. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value monasteries landscapes culture and peaceful Himalayan atmosphere. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value monasteries landscapes culture and peaceful Himalayan atmosphere. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value monasteries landscapes culture and peaceful

name: Lantau Island
description:
Lantau combines beaches, mountain trails, fishing villages, and cultural landmarks beyond the dense urban core. Top attractions include Tai O, hiking routes, and coastal scenery. Visitors can explore a very different side of Hong Kong. Key highlights include island diversity. Things to do include hiking and village exploration.

Lantau Island is the largest island in Hong Kong and offers a totally different vibe from the city—mountains, monasteries, fishing villages, and peaceful landscapes. It’s where you go to slow down and experience Hong Kong’s natural and cultural side.

Top Highlights

Tian Tan Buddha (Big Buddha)

  • Tian Tan Buddha
  • One of the world’s largest seated outdoor Buddha statues
  • Climb 268 steps for panoramic views
  • Symbol of harmony between humans and nature

Po Lin Monastery

  • Po Lin Monastery
  • A serene Buddhist complex near the Big Buddha
  • Famous for its vegetarian meals and peaceful atmosphere

🚠 Ngong Ping 360 Cable Car


  • Ngong Ping 360
  • One of Asia’s most scenic cable car rides
  • Sweeping views of mountains, sea, and even the airport
  • Option for glass-bottom “Crystal Cabin”

Tai O Fishing Village

  • Tai O
  • Known for traditional stilt houses over water
  • Try local snacks like salted fish & shrimp paste
  • Chance to spot pink dolphins nearby

Nature & Adventure

  • Lantau Peak – second-highest mountain in Hong Kong
  • Stunning sunrise hikes above the clouds 
  • Quiet beaches like Cheung Sha Beach

Bonus: Theme Park Nearby

  • Hong Kong Disneyland is also located on Lantau Island—easy to combine in a trip

How to Get There

  • MTR to Tung Chung Station (gateway to Lantau)
  • From there:
    • Cable car to Ngong Ping
    • Bus/taxi to Tai O or beaches

Best Time to Visit

  • October–April for comfortable weather
  • Early morning for fewer crowds at Big Buddha

Why Visit Lantau?

  • Spiritual landmarks 
  • Epic cable car views 
  • Authentic village life 
  • Scenic hikes & beaches 

Lantau Island feels like a different world from the skyscrapers—peaceful, cultural, and naturally stunning.

name: Hong Kong Disneyland
description:
Hong Kong Disneyland combines themed lands, entertainment, and family attractions in a compact resort setting. Top attractions include castle zones, rides, and shows. Visitors can enjoy a major themed destination. Key highlights include family appeal. Things to do include rides and performances.

Hong Kong Disneyland is a compact yet incredibly charming Disney park located on Lantau Island. It blends classic Disney magic with unique attractions you won’t find anywhere else—making it a must-visit for families, couples, and Disney fans.

What Makes It Special?

Castle of Magical Dreams

  • The park’s centerpiece—larger and more modern than before
  • Represents multiple Disney princess stories
  • Stunning backdrop for photos and nighttime shows

Unique Rides You Can’t Miss

  • Mystic Manor – one of Disney’s best trackless dark rides 
  • Iron Man Experience – Marvel-themed flight adventure 
  • Big Grizzly Mountain Runaway Mine Cars – part coaster, part story ride
  • Hyperspace Mountain – Star Wars-themed indoor coaster

Themed Lands

  • Main Street, U.S.A. – classic Disney entrance
  • Fantasyland – princesses, fairytales, gentle rides
  • Adventureland – jungle cruise & exploration
  • Tomorrowland – Marvel & futuristic attractions
  • Grizzly Gulch – Wild West vibes
  • Mystic Point – exclusive to Hong Kong Disneyland

Shows & Entertainment


  • “Momentous” Nighttime Spectacular 🎆
  • Parades with Disney characters
  • Stage shows like Festival of the Lion King

Food & Shopping

  • Themed restaurants (dim sum + international options)
  • Character dining experiences
  • Disney merchandise stores throughout the park

How to Get There

  • MTR: Disneyland Resort Line (easy transfer from Sunny Bay)
  • About 30–40 minutes from central Hong Kong

Best Time to Visit

  • Weekdays for shorter queues
  • November–March for cooler weather
  • Arrive early to maximize rides

Tips for Visiting

  • Use the Disney Premier Access pass for popular rides
  • Download the park app for wait times
  • Stay for the night show—it’s worth it

Ocean Park vs Disneyland (Quick Take)

  • Hong Kong Disneyland → Magic, storytelling, family-friendly rides
  • Ocean Park Hong Kong → Thrills, animals, nature views

Hong Kong Disneyland is all about immersive storytelling, fantasy worlds, and feel-good magic—a place where every corner feels like a movie scene.

name: Zadar
description: Zadar blends Roman remains, Venetian gates, and innovative waterfront design. Top attractions include Sea Organ, Greeting to the Sun, Roman Forum, St. Donatus Church, and sunset promenade views. Visitors can hear wave-powered music and explore historic streets. Key highlights include Adriatic sunsets and old-meets-modern character. Things to do include heritage walks and coastal excursions.

name: Las Vegas
description: Las Vegas combines resort spectacle with desert excursions. Top attractions include the Strip, themed resorts, shows, and nearby Red Rock Canyon. Visitors can explore nightlife, dining, and entertainment venues. Key highlights include illuminated architecture and nearby desert landscapes. Things to do include shows, scenic drives, and day trips to Hoover Dam.

name: Manali
description:
Manali is a picturesque hill station located in the Kullu Valley, known for its snow-capped mountains, adventure sports, and scenic landscapes. It serves as a gateway to the Solang Valley and Rohtang Pass. Visitors can enjoy activities like skiing, paragliding, and river rafting. The town also features attractions such as Hadimba Temple and Manu Temple. Old Manali offers a laid-back vibe with cafes and cultural experiences. During winter, Manali becomes a hub for snow sports, while summers attract nature lovers. Its stunning views, pleasant climate, and adventure opportunities make it one of the top destinations in Himachal Pradesh.

Set along the Beas River and surrounded by snow-capped peaks, Manali is one of India’s most popular hill stations—famous for adventure sports, scenic beauty, and cozy mountain vibes.

Why Manali is a favorite

  • Snowy landscapes and cool weather ❄️
  • Perfect mix of relaxation + adventure
  • Lively cafés and backpacker culture
  • Gateway to high-altitude regions like Spiti Valley

Top attractions

Hadimba Devi Temple

  • Ancient wooden temple surrounded by deodar forests
  • Peaceful and atmospheric

Solang Valley

  • Hub for adventure sports
  • Paragliding, skiing, zorbing, cable car rides

Rohtang Pass

  • Snow-covered landscapes and breathtaking views
  • Accessible mainly from May to October

Atal Tunnel

  • One of the world’s longest high-altitude tunnels
  • Connects Manali to Lahaul valley

Old Manali

  • Chill cafés, live music, backpacker vibe
  • Great place to relax and unwind

Things to do

  • Paragliding & skiing 
  • River rafting in the Beas 
  • Trekking to nearby valleys
  • Café hopping in Old Manali 
  • Snow activities in winter

Best time to visit

  • March–June: Pleasant summer getaway
  • October–February: Snowfall season 
  • July–September: Monsoon (less crowded but rainy)

How to reach

  • Nearest airport: Bhuntar (near Kullu)
  • Road: Well connected from Delhi and Chandigarh
  • Popular route: Delhi → Kullu → Manali

What to try

  • Trout fish (local specialty)
  • Himachali dishes like siddu and madra
  • International cuisine in Old Manali cafés

Who should go?

  • Couples & honeymooners 
  • Adventure seekers
  • First-time Himalayan travelers

name: Hong Kong
description: Hong Kong blends skyscrapers, harbors, and cultural diversity. Victoria Peak offers skyline views, while markets and temples reflect tradition. Cuisine, shopping, and ferry rides create a vibrant urban experience. The city’s global character sets it apart. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value history culture landscapes cuisine and vast geographic diversity across China. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value history culture landscapes cuisine and vast geographic diversity across China. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value history culture landscapes cuisine and vast geographic diversity across China. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value history culture landscapes cuisine and vast geographic diversity across China. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value history culture landscapes cuisine and vast geographic diversity across China. name: Sai Kung
description:
Sai Kung combines seafood town charm, geopark coastlines, and island-dotted waters. Top attractions include boat routes, beaches, and volcanic rock formations. Visitors can explore Hong Kong’s scenic outdoor side. Key highlights include coastal landscapes. Things to do include hiking and boating.

Sai Kung is one of the most scenic and laid-back areas in Hong Kong—a refreshing contrast to the city’s dense skyline. Known for its crystal-clear waters, hiking trails, and seafood culture, it’s a favorite escape for both locals and travelers.

Why Visit Sai Kung?

Natural Beauty

  • Often called the “back garden of Hong Kong”
  • Home to the stunning Hong Kong UNESCO Global Geopark
  • Famous for volcanic rock formations, sea caves, and turquoise waters

Island Adventures

  • Take a boat from Sai Kung Pier to:
    • Sharp Island (great beaches & snorkeling)
    • Kau Sai Chau (golf & coastal views)
    • High Island Reservoir (iconic hexagonal columns)

Seafood Paradise


  • Sai Kung is famous for its live seafood restaurants
  • Pick your seafood straight from tanks 🐟🦀
  • Popular dishes: chili crab, steamed fish, garlic prawns
  • Enjoy waterfront dining with a relaxed vibe

Things to Do

Hiking & Nature

  • MacLehose Trail (Stage 1–2) – coastal hikes with epic views
  • Beaches like Tai Long Wan offer white sand and clear water

Water Activities

  • Kayaking, snorkeling, and island hopping
  • Boat tours to hidden beaches and caves

Village Stroll

  • Walk along Sai Kung Promenade
  • Browse small shops, bakeries, and cafes

How to Get There

  • MTR to Choi Hung or Hang Hau, then minibus/bus to Sai Kung
  • About 1–1.5 hours from central Hong Kong

Best Time to Visit

  • October to April: Pleasant weather for hiking
  • Weekdays are quieter; weekends can get lively

Sai Kung is where Hong Kong slows down—perfect if you want nature, fresh seafood, and coastal charm all in one place.

name: Chashme Shahi
description: Chashme Shahi is a Mughal garden famous for its natural spring water. The garden is smaller compared to others but offers a serene environment. The water from the spring is believed to have medicinal properties. Visitors can enjoy the peaceful surroundings and scenic beauty. It is a perfect spot for relaxation and photography.

name: Raffles Hotel
description:
Raffles Hotel is a historic luxury hotel known for its colonial architecture and elegance. It is famous for the Singapore Sling cocktail. Visitors can explore its heritage and enjoy fine dining experiences.

The legendary Raffles Hotel Singapore is one of the world’s most iconic luxury hotels—famous for its colonial elegance, rich history, and timeless charm.

Why Raffles Hotel is special

  • Opened in 1887 during the British colonial era
  • A symbol of luxury and heritage in Singapore
  • Hosted famous guests like Rudyard Kipling and Charlie Chaplin
  • A perfect blend of history, architecture, and hospitality

Top Experiences

Long Bar

  • Birthplace of the famous Singapore Sling cocktail
  • Unique tradition: throw peanut shells on the floor

Stay in a Suite

  • All rooms are luxury suites
  • Classic colonial décor with modern comfort

Raffles Arcade

  • Boutique shopping and dining within the hotel

Dining

  • Fine dining restaurants 
  • Afternoon tea experiences 
  • Bars and cafés with historic ambiance

Things to do

  • Walk through the hotel’s beautiful courtyards
  • Enjoy drinks at the Long Bar
  • Experience afternoon tea
  • Explore nearby city attractions

Best time to visit

  • Afternoon for tea 
  • Evening for drinks and ambiance 

Location

  • Central Singapore, near:
    • Marina Bay
    • Little India

How long to spend

  • 1–2 hours for a visit
  • Longer if dining or staying

Travel tips

  • Dress smart-casual (especially for dining areas)
  • Visit even if not staying—it’s an experience
  • Try the original Singapore Sling 

Fun fact

Raffles Hotel is credited with creating the Singapore Sling in the early 1900s.

name: Satkosia Gorge
description: Satkosia Gorge is a scenic destination where the Mahanadi River flows through forested hills. It offers wildlife, boating, and camping experiences. Visitors can enjoy nature and adventure activities. The area is rich in biodiversity and natural beauty.

name: Thiksey Monastery
description: Thiksey Monastery is a beautiful monastery resembling the Potala Palace in Tibet. It is located on a hilltop and offers panoramic views. The monastery features a large statue of Maitreya Buddha and prayer halls. Visitors can attend morning prayers and explore the complex.

name: Mukachevo
description: Mukachevo combines hilltop castle heritage and Central European-influenced townscapes. Top attractions include Palanok Castle and old-town streets. Visitors can explore western Ukraine’s borderland cultural character. Key highlights include castle scenery. Things to do include heritage walks.

name: Kuakata
description: Kuakata is known as the daughter of the sea offering views of both sunrise and sunset over the Bay of Bengal Visitors can relax on beaches and explore nearby villages and temples Visitors enjoy rivers culture heritage landscapes history cuisine and vibrant local experiences throughout Bangladesh. Visitors enjoy rivers culture heritage landscapes history cuisine and vibrant local experiences throughout Bangladesh. Visitors enjoy rivers culture heritage landscapes history cuisine and vibrant local experiences throughout Bangladesh. name: Zakopane & Tatra Mountains
description: Zakopane combines wooden highland culture with access to the Tatra Mountains. Top attractions include Kasprowy Wierch, Morskie Oko, Gubałówka, and mountain trails. Visitors can hike alpine valleys, experience Gorals traditions, and enjoy dramatic peaks. Key highlights include mountain scenery and regional identity. Things to do include trekking, skiing, and scenic cable rides.

name: Prague Zoo
description:
Prague Zoo is one of the best zoos in Europe featuring diverse animal species and natural habitats It offers educational exhibits and family friendly experiences Visitors can explore themed areas and enjoy a day with wildlife Visitors enjoy medieval architecture castles bridges culture history cafés and vibrant city life throughout Prague. Visitors enjoy medieval architecture castles bridges culture history cafés and vibrant city life throughout Prague.

Prague Zoo is widely regarded as one of the top zoological parks in the world, known for its spacious natural habitats, conservation work, and unique hillside layout.

Location

  • In the Troja district of Prague
  • Along the banks of the Vltava River
  • Near Troja Palace

Why It’s Special

  • Ranked among the best zoos globally
  • Covers a large area with open, naturalistic enclosures
  • Strong focus on conservation and endangered species

Animals & Exhibits

Gorilla Pavilion

  • One of the zoo’s most famous sections
  • Home to a well-known gorilla family

Elephant Valley

  • Spacious habitat designed to mimic natural environments
  • One of the most advanced elephant enclosures in Europe

Big Cats & Other Wildlife

  • Tigers, lions, and leopards
  • Also reptiles, birds, and aquatic animals

Indonesian Jungle & Penguin Area

  • Tropical indoor environment
  • Walk through lush greenery with exotic species

Unique Experiences

  • Chairlift ride connecting lower and upper zoo areas
  • Offers scenic views of the zoo and surrounding landscape

Family-Friendly

  • Petting zoo areas
  • Playgrounds and educational programs
  • Restaurants and picnic spots

Best Time to Visit

  • Spring and autumn for pleasant weather
  • Morning to see animals more active

Travel Tips

  • Plan at least 3–5 hours (it’s large!)
  • Wear comfortable walking shoes
  • Use the chairlift to save energy

Why Visit Prague Zoo?

It’s not just a zoo—it’s a world-class wildlife experience, combining education, conservation, and beautifully designed habitats in a scenic setting.

name: Peleș Castle
description: Peleș Castle is known for ornate Neo-Renaissance interiors set against forested mountain slopes. Top attractions include castle rooms, terraced gardens, and nearby mountain scenery. Visitors can explore one of Europe’s most richly decorated royal residences. Key highlights include craftsmanship and alpine setting. Things to do include heritage visits and scenic excursions.

name: Ayodhya Ram Janmabhoomi
description: Ayodhya is believed to be the birthplace of Lord Rama and is a major pilgrimage destination. The Ram Janmabhoomi site holds immense religious importance. The city features temples, ghats, and spiritual landmarks. Visitors can explore its cultural heritage and participate in religious ceremonies. Ayodhya represents devotion and tradition.

name: Bundi
description: Bundi charms with narrow lanes, stepwells, and Taragarh Fort overlooking blue houses. Bundi Palace features murals and ornate rooms, while baoris like Rani Ji ki Baori display intricate stonework. The town feels less crowded, allowing slow exploration of architecture and daily life. Water bodies, gates, and gateways create a picturesque setting that reflects Rajasthan’s artistic traditions. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value history architecture culture and vivid desert landscapes. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value history architecture culture and vivid desert landscapes. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value history architecture culture and vivid desert landscapes. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value history architecture culture and vivid desert landscapes. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value history architecture culture and vivid desert landscapes. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value history architecture culture and vivid desert landscapes. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value history architecture culture and vivid desert landscapes. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value history architecture culture and vivid desert landscapes. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value history architecture culture and vivid desert landscapes. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value history

name: Nan Lian Garden
description:
Nan Lian Garden combines Tang-style landscaping, ponds, and quiet urban retreat spaces. Top attractions include landscaped gardens and nearby Chi Lin Nunnery. Visitors can experience refined traditional aesthetics in the city. Key highlights include contemplative design. Things to do include garden walks.

Nan Lian Garden is a beautifully designed classical Chinese garden in Hong Kong, offering a rare sense of calm, symmetry, and timeless elegance right in the middle of the city.

Where is it?

  • Located in Diamond Hill, Hong Kong
  • Right next to the famous Chi Lin Nunnery
  • Easily reached by MTR (Diamond Hill Station)

What makes it special?

Tang Dynasty design

  • Built in traditional Tang Dynasty style
  • No nails used in major wooden structures
  • Designed with precise harmony and balance

Perfectly landscaped beauty

  • Carefully arranged rocks, trees, and water features
  • Bonsai, lotus ponds, and manicured greenery
  • Every view feels like a painting

Peaceful escape

  • A quiet oasis away from Hong Kong’s busy streets
  • Ideal for relaxation, photography, and reflection

Top things to see

Golden Pavilion

  • The garden’s most iconic structure
  • Set beside a lotus pond with a red bridge

Lotus ponds & water features

  • Calm, reflective waters
  • Seasonal lotus blooms 

Bonsai & rock displays

  • Symbolic arrangements inspired by nature
  • Represent mountains and landscapes in miniature

Things to do

  • Take a peaceful walk through the garden
  • Visit Chi Lin Nunnery next door
  • Enjoy vegetarian food at the garden restaurant
  • Capture stunning photos 📸

Best time to visit

  • Morning or late afternoon: Quiet and serene 
  • Spring: Flowers and greenery at their best 

Travel tips

  • Free entry 
  • Maintain silence and respect the tranquil environment
  • Combine with nearby attractions for a half-day visit

Why visit?

Nan Lian Garden is a rare blend of art, nature, and philosophy—a perfectly balanced space that offers peace and beauty in one of the world’s busiest cities.

name: Baracoa
description: Baracoa combines rainforest mountains, rivers, and isolated Atlantic coast scenery. Top attractions include El Yunque, cacao farms, rivers, and black-sand beaches. Visitors can hike tropical trails and sample coconut-rich cuisine. Key highlights include remote landscapes and ecological diversity. Things to do include hiking, river excursions, and coastal exploration.

name: Edinburgh
description:
Edinburgh rises across volcanic ridges and medieval lanes crowned by its iconic castle. Top attractions include Edinburgh Castle, Royal Mile, Holyrood Palace, Arthur’s Seat, and Old Town closes. Visitors can walk atmospheric stone streets, climb panoramic viewpoints, and experience literary and festival traditions. Key highlights include dramatic city topography and layered Scottish history.

The historic capital of Scotland, Edinburgh, is one of Europe’s most captivating cities—famous for its medieval Old Town, elegant Georgian New Town, and dramatic skyline.

Why Edinburgh is special

  • UNESCO World Heritage-listed Old & New Towns
  • Rich history of castles, kings, and legends
  • A city of festivals, culture, and literature
  • Stunning mix of nature and architecture

Top Attractions

Edinburgh Castle

  • Perched on a volcanic rock
  • Iconic symbol of Scotland

Royal Mile

  • Historic street connecting the castle to the palace
  • Full of shops, pubs, and landmarks

Palace of Holyroodhouse

  • Official residence of the British monarch in Scotland

Arthur’s Seat

  • Ancient volcano offering panoramic city views

Festivals & Culture

  • Famous for the Edinburgh Festival Fringe
  • Also hosts:
    • Edinburgh International Festival
    • Hogmanay (New Year celebrations) 🎆

Things to do

  • Explore medieval streets and hidden alleys
  • Visit museums and galleries
  • Hike Arthur’s Seat 🥾
  • Enjoy traditional Scottish pubs

Food & Experience

  • Scottish cuisine:
    • Haggis
    • Fish & chips 🐟
  • Whisky tasting experiences 🥃

Best time to visit

  • May–September: best weather 🌿
  • August: festival season (very lively)
  • Winter: magical festive atmosphere ❄️

Getting there

  • Well connected by train and air
  • About 4.5 hours from London

How long to stay

  • 2–3 days for highlights
  • 4–5 days with nearby exploration

Travel tips

  • Book accommodation early during festivals
  • Wear comfortable shoes (hilly streets)
  • Be prepared for changing weather

Fun fact

Edinburgh is often considered one of the most haunted cities in the world 👻.

name: Tiger Cave Temple
description:
Tiger Cave Temple is a famous Buddhist temple known for its challenging climb of over 1,200 steps. The effort is rewarded with panoramic views of Krabi’s landscape. The temple features caves, statues, and a large golden Buddha at the summit. It is a place of spiritual significance and meditation. Visitors experience both physical challenge and cultural insight. The temple is a must-visit for those seeking adventure and spirituality.

Tiger Cave Temple (Wat Tham Suea) is one of Krabi’s most famous spiritual and scenic attractions. It is known for its cave temple, meditation grounds, and the dramatic 1,260-step climb to a hilltop summit crowned by a giant golden Buddha.

Highlights

  • Hilltop viewpoint with sweeping views of Krabi’s limestone landscape
  • Giant golden Buddha statue at the summit
  • Sacred cave temple and monastery complex
  • Peaceful meditation areas and jungle surroundings

Things to do

  • Climb the 1,260 steps to the summit (challenging but rewarding)
  • Explore the cave temple and shrines below
  • Watch for monkeys around the grounds 🐒
  • Enjoy sunrise or sunset views from the top
  • Experience a quieter spiritual side of Krabi beyond the beaches

Tips for visiting

  • Go early morning or late afternoon (cooler temperatures)
  • Bring water and wear proper footwear
  • The staircase is steep—take your time
  • Dress respectfully since it is an active Buddhist temple

Why it’s called “Tiger Cave”

Local stories say tigers once lived in the caves, and some believe rock formations resemble tiger paws—hence the name Tiger Cave Temple.

Why visit

It’s one of the best places in Krabi for a mix of culture, adventure, and panoramic views. The summit climb is tough, but many consider it one of Thailand’s most rewarding temple hikes.

name: Desert Safari
description:
Dubai Desert Safari is one of the most exciting and must-do experiences in Dubai. It takes you into the vast Arabian desert for a mix of adventure, culture, and entertainment.

What Makes It Special

  • Thrilling desert activities combined with traditional Bedouin-style experiences
  • Stunning sunset views over golden sand dunes
  • A perfect blend of adrenaline + culture

Adventure Activities

Dune Bashing

  • High-speed ride over sand dunes in a 4x4 vehicle
  • The most popular and thrilling part of the safari

Sandboarding

  • Slide down dunes like snowboarding
  • Fun and beginner-friendly

Camel Riding

  • Short rides offering a taste of traditional desert travel

Cultural Experiences

Desert Camp

  • Traditional Arabian-style camp setup
  • Henna painting, shisha, and local dress photography

Live Entertainment

  • Belly dance, Tanoura dance, and fire shows
  • Cultural performances under the stars

BBQ Dinner

  • Buffet dinner with vegetarian and non-vegetarian options
  • Includes Middle Eastern dishes and desserts

Types of Safaris

  • Morning Safari – More adventure-focused, less crowded
  • Evening Safari – Most popular (sunset + dinner + shows)
  • Overnight Safari – Camping in the desert with stargazing

Location

  • Conducted in desert areas outside Dubai (like Lahbab or Al Awir)
  • Usually includes hotel pickup and drop-off

Best Time to Go

  • Evening for the complete experience
  • Winter months (Nov–Mar) for pleasant weather

Tips for Visitors

  • Wear light, comfortable clothes and sunglasses
  • Avoid heavy meals before dune bashing
  • Carry a light jacket for cooler evenings

Why It’s a Must-Do

  • One of the most authentic and thrilling UAE experiences
  • Combines adventure, culture, and food in one trip
  • Perfect for families, couples, and solo travelers.

name: Anse Source d’Argent
description: Anse Source d’Argent is famous for pale sand, shallow lagoons, and granite formations shaped into extraordinary natural sculptures. Top attractions include lagoon coves and reef-protected shallows. Visitors can experience one of Seychelles’ most iconic landscapes. Key highlights include vivid water color and dramatic boulder scenery. Things to do include swimming, snorkeling, and photography.

name: Ait Ben Haddou
description: Ait Ben Haddou is a UNESCO-listed ksar known for mud-brick towers, hilltop granaries, and film locations. Top attractions include the fortified village, panoramic viewpoints, Atlas Studios, and nearby Taourirt Kasbah. Visitors can explore ancient alleys, climb to the upper granary, and learn about caravan trade routes. Key highlights include sunrise photography, earthen architecture, and cinematic heritage. Things to do include guided walks and combining the site with High Atlas or desert journeys.

name: Banff National Park
description: Banff National Park is defined by turquoise glacial lakes, snow-capped peaks, and scenic drives through the Canadian Rockies. Top attractions include Lake Louise, Moraine Lake, the Icefields Parkway, Johnston Canyon, and Banff Gondola. Visitors can hike alpine trails, paddle on glacier-fed lakes, and watch wildlife such as elk and bighorn sheep. Key highlights include mountain panoramas, reflective lake scenery, and year-round outdoor adventure. Things to do include hiking, canoeing, skiing, and scenic road trips.

name: Xi’an
description: Xi’an is known for the Terracotta Army, an extraordinary archaeological discovery of life-sized soldiers guarding an emperor’s tomb. The ancient city walls and Muslim Quarter add cultural layers. As the start of the Silk Road, Xi’an offers historical depth and diverse influences. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value history culture landscapes cuisine and vast geographic diversity across China. name: Bali

name: Anse Intendance
description: Anse Intendance reveals a wilder side of Seychelles with surf, deep-blue water, and a broad palm-backed shoreline. Top attractions include the beach and surrounding viewpoints. Visitors can experience a less sheltered, more dramatic coastal landscape. Key highlights include powerful ocean scenery. Things to do include beach walks and photography.

name: Delhi Red Fort
description: A historic Mughal fort and UNESCO site with massive red sandstone walls.

name: Visby
description: Visby preserves stone walls, merchant lanes, and church ruins tied to Hanseatic trade. Top attractions include city walls, old town streets, and rose-filled alleys. Visitors can experience one of northern Europe’s finest medieval towns. Key highlights include preserved urban heritage. Things to do include walking tours and cultural events.

name: Lhasa
description: Lhasa is the spiritual center of Tibetan Buddhism, featuring the Potala Palace and Jokhang Temple. Pilgrims walk around sacred sites, creating a unique atmosphere. High altitude and mountain surroundings add to its distinct character. Lhasa offers deep cultural and spiritual insights. The experience remains memorable for visitors who value history culture landscapes cuisine and vast geographic diversity across China. name: Adalaj Stepwell
description: Adalaj Stepwell is a five-story stepwell known for its intricate carvings and architectural beauty. Built in the 15th century, it served as a water source and resting place. The stepwell remains cool even during hot weather. It is a popular tourist attraction and a fine example of ancient engineering.

name: Lake Como
description:
Lake Como is known for its stunning scenery, villas, and mountains. Boat rides connect charming towns like Bellagio and Varenna. The lake offers a peaceful retreat with beautiful landscapes and elegant architecture. It is popular for relaxation and scenic exploration.

The breathtaking Lake Como (Lago di Como) is one of Italy’s most glamorous and scenic destinations, surrounded by the Alps and dotted with charming villages and luxury villas.

Why Lake Como is special

  • Stunning mix of mountains + crystal-clear lake views
  • Famous for luxury villas and celebrity retreats
  • Peaceful atmosphere compared to big cities
  • One of the deepest lakes in Europe

Top Places Around Lake Como

Bellagio

  • Known as the “Pearl of Lake Como”
  • Cobblestone streets, boutiques, and lakefront views

Villa del Balbianello

  • Iconic villa featured in films like Star Wars Episode II: Attack of the Clones
  • Beautiful gardens and panoramic views

Varenna

  • Romantic and quieter than Bellagio
  • Colorful houses along the water

Como

  • Main town with shops, cafés, and transport links
  • Gateway to the lake

Things to do

  • Take a boat cruise across the lake
  • Visit historic villas and gardens
  • Ride the funicular in Como for panoramic views
  • Enjoy lakeside dining and sunset views

Food & Experience

  • Fresh fish from the lake
  • Italian wines and lakeside cafés
  • Relaxed, romantic atmosphere

Best time to visit

  • April–June & September: best weather and fewer crowds
  • Summer: lively but busy and expensive

Getting there

  • About 1 hour from Milan by train or car

Travel tips

  • Stay in Bellagio or Varenna for the best views
  • Use ferries to explore different towns
  • Book accommodation early in peak season

Fun fact

Lake Como is a favorite getaway for celebrities like George Clooney, who owns a villa here.

name: Chitrakoot
description: Chitrakoot is a sacred town associated with Lord Rama’s exile. It features temples, waterfalls, and scenic landscapes. Visitors can explore Ram Ghat and Kamadgiri Hill. The town offers a blend of spirituality and natural beauty.

name: Pasighat
description: Pasighat is one of the oldest towns in Arunachal Pradesh, located on the banks of the Siang River. It offers scenic beauty and adventure activities like river rafting. Visitors can explore nearby villages and experience local culture. Pasighat is a gateway to the eastern Himalayas.

name: Camagüey
description: Camagüey is known for winding streets designed to confuse pirates and plazas hidden across the city. Top attractions include Plaza San Juan de Dios, cathedral zones, and artisan quarters. Visitors can explore colonial courtyards and clay pottery traditions. Key highlights include maze-like urban layout and heritage architecture. Things to do include walking tours and cultural exploration.

name: Rangamati
description: Rangamati is a hill district known for Kaptai Lake and surrounding landscapes Visitors can enjoy boat rides tribal culture and scenic views The area offers a mix of nature and cultural experiences Visitors enjoy rivers culture heritage landscapes history cuisine and vibrant local experiences throughout Bangladesh. Visitors enjoy rivers culture heritage landscapes history cuisine and vibrant local experiences throughout Bangladesh. Visitors enjoy rivers culture heritage landscapes history cuisine and vibrant local experiences throughout Bangladesh. name: Drvengrad & Šargan Eight
description: Drvengrad and the Šargan Eight combine a traditional-style wooden village with a historic narrow-gauge railway looping mountain slopes. Top attractions include heritage train rides and village streets. Visitors can experience cinematic architecture and scenic rail engineering. Key highlights include mountain rail heritage. Things to do include train excursions and village exploration.

name: Tsagaan Suvarga
description: Tsagaan Suvarga features eroded white-and-ochre cliffs resembling an ancient city wall. Top attractions include escarpment viewpoints and surrounding desert routes. Visitors can experience striking geological scenery. Key highlights include layered formations and color contrasts. Things to do include photography.

name: Azores
description:
The Azores offer crater lakes, geothermal springs, and dramatic Atlantic landscapes. Top attractions include Sete Cidades, Furnas, Lagoa do Fogo, and volcanic coastlines. Visitors can hike calderas, bathe in thermal waters, and watch whales offshore. Key highlights include volcanic nature and biodiversity.

The Azores are a stunning group of nine volcanic islands scattered across the Atlantic Ocean—often described as Europe’s answer to Hawaii. Think crater lakes, hot springs, waterfalls, and wild green landscapes with very few crowds.

Where are they?

  • In the Atlantic Ocean, about 1,500 km west of Portugal
  • Divided into three island groups: Eastern, Central, and Western

What makes the Azores special?

Volcanic landscapes

  • Massive crater lakes like Sete Cidades
  • Active geothermal areas with steam vents and hot springs
  • Dramatic cliffs and lava fields

Ocean adventures

  • One of the best whale-watching destinations in the world 
  • Dolphins, sperm whales, and blue whales
  • Natural swimming pools formed by volcanic rock

Untouched nature

  • Lush greenery, hydrangea-lined roads, waterfalls
  • Less commercialized than many island destinations

Main islands to explore

São Miguel (The “Green Island”)

  • Most popular and easiest to reach
  • Highlights: Sete Cidades, Furnas hot springs, Lagoa do Fogo

Pico Island

  • Home to Mount Pico
  • Famous for volcanic vineyards (UNESCO-listed)

Terceira

  • Historic town of Angra do Heroísmo (UNESCO site)
  • Cultural festivals and colorful architecture

Flores Island

  • Known for waterfalls, cliffs, and wild beauty
  • One of the most remote and scenic islands

Top things to do

  • Hike volcanic trails through lush landscapes
  • Soak in hot springs (especially in Furnas)
  • Go whale watching 
  • Swim in natural lava pools
  • Drive scenic coastal roads

Best time to visit

  • May–September: Best weather 
  • June–July: Hydrangeas in bloom 
  • Winter: Mild but wetter and unpredictable

Travel tips

  • Weather changes quickly—pack layers 🌦️
  • Renting a car is highly recommended 🚗
  • Flights usually connect via Lisbon or Porto

Why visit?

The Azores are perfect if you want raw nature, adventure, and tranquility—a place where volcanic power meets lush beauty, without mass tourism.

name: Mount Kilimanjaro
description: Kilimanjaro rises through cultivated foothills, cloud forests, and alpine deserts to Africa’s highest summit. Top attractions include trekking routes such as Machame and Marangu, moorland zones, and glacier views near the summit. Visitors can experience a legendary mountain ascent through striking ecological zones. Key highlights include altitude diversity and iconic peak landscapes. Things to do include trekking and photography.

name: Kalyan Minaret
description: The Kalyan Minaret is a towering structure that has stood for centuries in Bukhara. It was used as a beacon and a place of call to prayer. The minaret’s design and height make it a prominent feature of the city skyline. It is part of a larger architectural complex including a mosque and madrasa. Visitors enjoy Silk Road history architecture culture mosques madrasas bazaars and rich heritage throughout Uzbekistan. Visitors enjoy Silk Road history architecture culture mosques madrasas bazaars and rich heritage throughout Uzbekistan.
